---
title: "About DECK#"
format-links: false
---

**DECK#** (pronounced "Deck Sharp") is an open-source standardized annotation system for regular playing cards (52 + 2 Jokers), created by [Roberto Bisceglie](https://github.com/zeruhur).

It is a layered protocol enabling complex tabletop games using only a standard deck and two permanent markers (black and red). No proprietary components, no dedicated materials, no dependency on specific publishers or manufacturers.

## Philosophy

DECK# is inspired by **Piecepack** — an open component system for board games designed by James Kyle in 2000. Like Piecepack, DECK# provides a standardized, open substrate on which the community freely builds games.

DECK# defines vocabulary and structure only. Individual games define semantics. The system is language-agnostic: the entire graphic vocabulary consists of geometric glyphs and Arabic numerals.

## Design Invariants

- Only 2 markers are used (black + red); glyph color is never a free choice
- The glyph vocabulary is exactly 7 primary symbols
- The card back may only carry identical quadrant markers
- Annotations are additive — they never replace the substrate (suit/color/value)

## License

DECK# is released under [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). You are free to share and adapt the material for any purpose, provided appropriate credit is given.

## Source

Full source is available on [GitHub](https://github.com/zeruhur/decksharp).

## Version

Current version: **0.7.0** (Draft)

---

*Questa pagina è disponibile solo in inglese. Per la documentazione in italiano, vedere il menu **Italiano** nella barra di navigazione.*
